concerns.text: Tasks T014 and T016 define the primary baseline as 'Non-COVID, Non-Flu' (excluding Influenza). This silently weakens FR-001 and US-2 which mandate a 'Non-COVID' group (all other vaccines) for the primary comparison. The plan's deviation to exclude Flu from the primary baseline contradicts the spec's explicit requirement for a 'Non-COVID' baseline, risking invalidation of the main ROR/PRR/IC calculations.

Tasks T042 and T045 mandate implementing logic to calculate and verify SC-005 ('proportion of signals satisfying 2-out-of-3 rule'). However, SC-005 is explicitly marked '[deferred]' in the spec. Implementing logic for a deferred success criterion violates the constraint preservation principle by forcing implementation of a metric the spec author explicitly chose to defer.

Task T036 places the 'consistency check' for the 2-out-of-3 rule inside `src/analysis/temporal.py` (US3). FR-005 and US-2 define signal validation as a core part of the Disproportionality Analysis (US2). Moving this validation to the Temporal module decouples the primary statistical engine from its validation logic, violating the semantic integrity of FR-005.

Task T024b implements a 'Background Rate Unknown' flag but fails to specify the source of truth for 'published background incidence rates' required by the spec's Edge Cases. Without defining the reference source (e.g., specific CDC report or literature), the task cannot verify the 'Unknown' status, creating a semantic gap in the Edge Case handling requirement.
